User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the QuestGenie application, typically a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: A unique username for the user, used for identification within the application.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

StudyMaterial.title: StudyMaterial
StudyMaterial.description: Represents the raw text or notes provided by a user to generate flashcards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyMaterial entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this study material. (Relationship: User 1:N StudyMaterial)

title.description: A descriptive title for the study material.

content.description: The actual text content or notes provided by the user for flashcard generation.

language.description: The language of the study material content (e.g., 'en', 'fr').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the study material was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the study material was last updated.

FlashcardSet.title: FlashcardSet
FlashcardSet.description: Represents a collection of related flashcards generated from a specific study material.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FlashcardSet entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this flashcard set. (Relationship: User 1:N FlashcardSet)

studyMaterialId.description: Reference to the StudyMaterial from which this set was generated. (Relationship: StudyMaterial 1:N FlashcardSet)

name.description: A user-defined name for this flashcard set.

description.description: An optional description for the flashcard set.

aiGenerated.description: Indicates if this flashcard set was initially generated by AI.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the flashcard set was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the flashcard set was last updated.

Flashcard.title: Flashcard
Flashcard.description: Represents a single question-and-answer pair within a flashcard set.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Flashcard entity.

flashcardSetId.description: Reference to the FlashcardSet this card belongs to. (Relationship: FlashcardSet 1:N Flashcard)

question.description: The question side of the flashcard.

answer.description: The answer side of the flashcard.

order.description: The display order of the flashcard within its set.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the flashcard was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the flashcard was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches the {userId} wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores study materials (raw text/notes) uploaded by a user. Ownership is determined by the parent {userId} in the path. Documents also contain a 'userId' field matching the path parameter for redundancy and queryability.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this study material.

params.name: studyMaterialId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific study material document.

definition.description: Stores sets of flashcards generated for a user. Ownership is determined by the parent {userId} in the path. Documents also contain a 'userId' field matching the path parameter and a 'studyMaterialId' linking to the source material.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this flashcard set.

params.name: flashcardSetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific flashcard set document.

definition.description: Stores individual flashcards belonging to a specific flashcard set. Ownership is implicitly inherited from the parent {userId} in the path, ensuring authorization independence for rules. The document contains a 'flashcardSetId' to link to its parent set.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent flashcard set and this flashcard.

params.name: flashcardSetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent flashcard set.

params.name: flashcardId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific flashcard document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** All collections are designed with path-based authorization, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For user-specific data, documents are placed directly under a `/users/{userId}/` path. This ensures that the `userId` is directly available in the `request.auth.uid` and in the path wildcard, making rules atomic and easy to debug. For instance, a `Flashcard` document at `/users/{userId}/flashcardSets/{flashcardSetId}/flashcards/{flashcardId}` is automatically owned by `{userId}` because the `userId` is an ancestor in the path. This means a rule only needs to check `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the path segment), rather than fetching the parent `flashcardSet` document to determine its owner.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The hierarchical path structure `users/{userId}/studyMaterials/{studyMaterialId}/flashcardSets/{flashcardSetId}/flashcards/{flashcardId}` explicitly states ownership and relationships. Each document's parentage is clear, making it obvious who should have access.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** Roles are not required for this application as all data is user-private. Authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid` matching the `userId` in the path.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The path-based authorization strategy inherently supports QAPs. When a user queries `/users/{request.auth.uid}/studyMaterials`, the query is automatically scoped to their own materials, and the security rules can enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` in the path, preventing unauthorized `list` operations. Similarly for `flashcardSets` and `flashcards` under the user's hierarchy. There are no public collections where users might accidentally expose data via `list` operations.

**Invariants:** The structure supports ownership invariants by placing all user-owned data under the respective user's path. Timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`) are standard fields within each entity, supporting data integrity. No complex denormalization for authorization is needed beyond what the path already provides, simplifying data consistency.

**Structural Segregation:** All documents within a given collection (`studyMaterials`, `flashcardSets`, `flashcards`) will share the same security posture (owned by the parent user), simplifying rule sets and preventing mixed-access data within the same collection.
